## [39:17 - 40:57]

what I usually do is take one to two of the posts I would normally do in the group and then put it on my personal profile. So I'll look at what I have planned for the week. And I'll be like, OK, well, let's do this value post on my personal this week. And then maybe if I'm feeling extra ambitious, I'll throw another case study post on my personal profile. That's kind of weird. So I don't know what that is. I still do both. I still think the group is 100% worth it, especially the group is way better for live trainings, typically a way better engagement. You get all the Legion opportunities with it. So don't not do the group. The group is amazing. And then also, there's a little bit of a flywheel going on. Because the more you build the group, you only have 5,000 friends. So the more you build your group, you can have 100,000 people in the group. And a lot of those people will follow you on your Facebook personal. So by building the group, you also build your personal and then vice versa, if you use your personal to drive to the group. So that's it for this training, guys.
